assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed-cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
out changes on each rising clk edge after reset according to LFSR polynomial.
No X values appear on out during observation.
cases.goal: Observe deterministic LFSR progression after reset.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
Conservative due to unknown latency; only external behavior is checked.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset and observe several clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe at least 5 rising edges post-reset.

expected_properties: After reset deassertion, out updates on each rising clk edge following XOR(out[3], out[2]) inverted feedback rule.
out never becomes X during observation window.
cases.goal: Validate correct LFSR state transition on clock edges after reset.
notes: Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
preconditions: Reset has been applied and released.
stimulus_intent: Assert reset high, then release it, then generate a sequence of rising clock edges.
timing_assumptions: Observe at least 4 consecutive rising edges after reset release.

expected_properties: When clk remains low, out retains its value across observation cycles.
cases.goal: Confirm that LFSR does not change state without clock activity.
notes: Case relies on deterministic clock-driven observation because no non-control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Hold clk low for multiple cycles after reset release.
timing_assumptions: No rising edge of clk occurs during the observation window.

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: Out remains defined (no X) even if reset is asserted while clk toggles.
cases.goal: Assess robustness to potentially illegal reset timing.
notes: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
stimulus_intent: Assert rst high while generating clock toggles.
